assignment07

Author

Xiaohan Xie

Part 1

In the same dataset of ATAC on chr19 used in the first part of the practical, plot 1) the insertion (i.e. ‘cuts’) profile of nucleosome-free fragments and 2) the centers of nucleosome-containing fragments, around the high-confidence motifs of a transcription factor of your choice (one figure containing the two signals) Transcription factor chosen: REST

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(epiwraps)
  library(AnnotationHub)
  library(MotifDb)
  library(memes)
  library(universalmotif)
  library(ensembldb)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(sechm)
  library(BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10)
  library(limma)
  library(betterChromVAR)
})
Warning: package 'dbplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
See system.file("LICENSE", package="MotifDb") for use restrictions.
Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
ah <- AnnotationHub(localHub=TRUE)
Using 'localHub=TRUE'
  If offline, please also see BiocManager vignette section on offline use
ensdb <- ah[["AH89211"]] # mouse ensembldb object
loading from cache

Obtaining the sites with a REST motif

# get CTCF motif
motif <- MotifDb::query(MotifDb, c("REST","Mus"))[[1]]
motif2 <- convert_motifs(motif, class="TFBSTools-PFMatrix")
Note: motif [motif] has an empty nsites slot, using 100.
genome <- ah[["AH68356"]]
loading from cache
# get the sequence for chr19:
chr19 <- import(genome)["19"]

# find motif matches across chr19
moi <- motifmatchr::matchMotifs(motif2, chr19, out="positions", p.cutoff=1e-5)[[1]]
# convert to GRanges
moi <- as(setNames(moi,names(chr19)), "GRanges")

Plotting signal around the motif occurences

# we prepare the list of tracks
tracks <- list.files(pattern="bw$")
names(tracks) <- gsub("\\.bw","",basename(tracks))

# extract signals around the motif occurences
# we zoom in to 300bp around the motif centers, in windows of 5bp
sm <- signal2Matrix(tracks, moi, w=5, extend=300)
Reading full_cov.bw
Reading full_cuts.bw
Reading mono_centers.bw
Reading mono_cov.bw
Reading NF_cov.bw
Reading NF_cuts.bw
# plot the signals:
plotEnrichedHeatmaps(sm, trim=0.95, multiScale = TRUE, axis_name = "0", use_raster = FALSE)
d <- meltSignals(sm[,c("NF_cuts", "mono_centers")])
ggplot(d, aes(position, mean, colour=sample)) + geom_line(size=1.2)
Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.

Part 2

In the peak counts dataset from stressed brains that we used in the second part of the practical, identify motifs that change in accessibility between males and females, and plot a heatmap of the deviations for the top few candidates motifs.

se <- readRDS("peakCounts.rds")

Annotate regions

# we restrict the regions to standard chromosomes and change the seq level style to match the genome
se <- GenomeInfoDb::keepStandardChromosomes(se, pruning.mode = "coarse")
seqlevelsStyle(se) <- "UCSC"
se <- addGCBias(se, genome=BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10)
# (to use a fasta file as genome, we could simply do genome=FaFile("path/to/genome.fasta") )

Motif matches

# make sure you specify a collection, in order to avoid having redundant motifs:
motifs <- MotifDb::query(MotifDb, "HOCOMOCOv13")
motifs <- do.call(TFBSTools::PWMatrixList, convert_motifs(motifs, class="TFBSTools-PWMatrix"))
names(motifs) <- make.unique(sapply(motifs, \(x) gsub("\\..+","",x@name)))

matches <- motifmatchr::matchMotifs(motifs, se, genome=BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10)

Get Motif deviations

dev <- betterChromVAR(se, matches)

Differential Analysis between Males and Females

dev$group <- ifelse(grepl("female", colnames(dev)), "Female", "Male")
design <- model.matrix(~group, data=as.data.frame(colData(dev)))
fit <- eBayes(lmFit(assay(dev, "z"), design))
res <- as.data.frame(topTable(fit, number = Inf))
Removing intercept from test coefficients
res$motif <- row.names(res)
ggplot(res, aes(logFC, -log10(adj.P.Val), label=motif)) + geom_text()
sechm(dev, head(row.names(res)), assayName = "z")
